LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BDO USA, LLP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF BRANDON BELANGER'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IN LIEU OF FILING A SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION TO THIS DEMURRER, MR. BELANGER FILED A RESPONSE REQUESTING LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED PLEADING, WHICH IS GRANTED, PER CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael Welch of the law offices of Michael Welch (415-399-1500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Katherine J. Liao of the law offices of DLA Piper US, LLP (310-595-3114) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/demurring party

Attorney, Susan Yee of the law offices of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC (415-442-4810) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

DEFENDANT BDO USA, LLP's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the first cause of action in plaintiff Brandon Belanger's first amended complaint is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend. In lieu of filing a substantive opposition to this demurrer, Mr. Belanger filed a response requesting leave to file an amended pleading, which is granted, per California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael Welch of the law offices of Michael Welch (415-399-1500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Katherine J. Liao of the law offices of DLA Piper US, LLP (310-595-3114) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/demurring party

Attorney, Susan Yee of the law offices of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC (415-442-4810) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

DEFENDANT KPMG LLP's (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS KPMG US LLP) DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the three causes of action in plaintiff Brandon Belanger's first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. In lieu of filing a substantive opposition to this demurrer, Mr. Belanger filed a response requesting leave to file an amended pleading, which is granted, per California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

No appearances.
